profile: M.Sc. Computer Science student at TU Darmstadt with 3+ years of startup experience across backend, full-stack, and data-heavy product work. Built high-throughput Java/Spring Boot APIs, server-driven React and TypeScript interfaces, ETL-style ingestion flows, internal automation, and AI-assisted workflows in fintech and web environments. Strong in SQL, API integrations, structured debugging, documentation, cross-functional delivery, and production reliability, with quantified wins on latency, error rate, reporting speed, rollout safety, and provider onboarding. Currently specializing in Data Science and Engineering and expanding into workflow orchestration, automation tooling, and LLM-based pipelines.

bullets: Owned Java/Spring Boot loan APIs end-to-end; improved p99 latency from 820 ms to 510 ms (-38%) and reduced 5xx rate from 1.8% to 1.0% (-44%) while sustaining about 270 QPS with peaks around 500.

Built a config-driven adapter framework for lender, KYC, and credit-bureau APIs; onboarded 10+ providers and reduced time-to-integrate from weeks to about 3 to 4 days for new providers and a few hours for variants.
Shipped a rule engine and async aggregation flow for financing offers; improved time-to-first-offer from 1.20 s to 0.78 s (-35%) and contributed to about 18% higher conversion.
Hardened reporting and Kafka data sync by reducing per-report Mongo queries from 100+ to fewer than 10, cutting report p95 from 3.1 to 2.0 minutes (-35%) and lowering Mongo CPU by about 40%.

Built a server-driven React + TypeScript UI configuration pattern so product teams could change fields, copy, and validation rules without redeploys, saving about 10 hours per update and reducing frontend-backend contract mismatches.
Shipped user-facing features end-to-end by integrating React clients with REST APIs and documented UI patterns and API usage to improve developer experience and release stability.

Built Python scripts for CSV and Excel ingestion, transformation, validation, upserts, audit trails, data preparation, and log parsing to reduce repetitive manual work and accelerate reporting and issue triage.
Automated multi-step data flows using schedulers, SFTP, Kafka, queues, and internal processing jobs to support near-real-time operations and reduce manual handoffs.
Implemented feature flags, caching, tests, and incremental rollouts to support safer experimentation, faster rollback, and more stable production releases.
Performed API load and reliability analysis with Postman, investigated failures from logs and metrics, documented mitigations, and drove high-priority fixes through handover and release.
Partnered closely with QA, product, operations, and external API providers to define contracts, clarify requirements, improve test coverage, and deliver reliable integrations.
Led code reviews, mentored 4 junior engineers, and maintained clear internal documentation, release notes, and handover material across backend and frontend workflows.
Reverse-engineered insurer APIs via Chrome DevTools network traces and built lightweight JavaScript mocks for faster backend integration work.
Authored OpenAPI / Swagger specifications and Postman collections to standardize contracts and speed up safer integrations across teams.
Tested and debugged backend APIs and supported stable releases by validating request and response behavior across services.
Built Python helpers for schema and payload validation and documented common request and response flows for smoother developer handover.
Supported UI iterations by keeping backend-frontend contracts consistent during integration and release preparation.
Restructured a multi-page website and improved performance and reliability using Lighthouse, GTmetrix, and Pingdom.
Delivered responsive UI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript while coordinating closely with project managers on change requests and fixes.
Optimized assets, CSS, and JavaScript, increasing user reach by about 60% and improving delivery efficiency by about 45% in tailored resume variants.

projects.name: Expense Management Automation Bot
bullets: Built a self-hosted assistant that orchestrates webhooks, database I/O, and multiple AI agents to categorize expenses and produce structured reports.
Added prompt-trimming guardrails and reduced SaaS spend by about 75% after migrating the workflow to Railway.
Chained text and audio parsing, routing, and reporting steps across numerous nodes to support practical automation-first workflows.

projects.name: Grammar Learning Assistant
projects.tech: AI workflows, quiz generation, review tracking
bullets: Built a study companion that generates meanings, examples, and spaced-practice quizzes while maintaining a review backlog.
Automated word capture and progress tracking to support repeated review and self-study.

projects.name: CSV Ingestion and ETL Toolkit
bullets: Built reusable utilities for schema checks, type validation, CSV and Excel ingestion, transformation, and database upserts.
Improved ad hoc data quality, repeatability, and turnaround time for reporting and internal analysis workflows.

projects.name: CO2 Reduction Gamification App
bullets: Built an AI-powered web app during a hackathon to help users reduce their carbon footprint through personalized and gamified tasks.
Used JavaScript, LangChain, OpenAI, and a weighted scoring model to adapt challenges and rewards to different user personas.

projects.name: Self-Driving Car Simulation Using Deep Learning
bullets: Implemented lane-line detection and traffic-sign classification using computer vision and convolutional neural networks.
Validated autonomous navigation behavior in the Udacity simulator after training and optimizing the model pipeline.
